nodes.name: calendars.ts
nodes.summary: Express request handlers for calendar CRUD and membership management (create, update, remove, join, leave, kick, list members, set roles), backed by data-access helpers, permission checks, and SSE member notifications.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zod (CalendarSchema.parse) for request validation and pushes real-time updates to members via notifyCalendarMembers.

nodes.name: handlerCreateCalendar
nodes.summary: Validates the request body against CalendarSchema and creates a new calendar, returning it with 201.

nodes.name: handlerRemoveCalendar
nodes.summary: Deletes a calendar after checking delete permission, then notifies all affected members over SSE.

nodes.name: handlerUpdateCalendar
nodes.summary: Validates and updates a calendar's fields after an edit-permission check, then broadcasts the change to members.

nodes.name: handlerGetCalendars
nodes.summary: Returns all calendars for the current user, enriched with member lists and external share links.

nodes.name: handlerGetCalendarFromToken
nodes.summary: Resolves a calendar from a share token and returns it with its members and events for public/link access.

nodes.name: handlerGetCalendar
nodes.summary: Fetches a single calendar by id along with its mapped member list.

nodes.name: handlerJoinCalendar
nodes.summary: Adds the current user as a member of a calendar resolved from a share token.

nodes.name: handlerLeaveCalendar
nodes.summary: Removes the current user's membership from a calendar.

nodes.name: handlerKickMember
nodes.summary: Removes another member from a calendar after a permission check, then notifies remaining members.

nodes.name: handlerGetCalendarMembers
nodes.summary: Returns the members of a calendar along with the requesting user's role.

nodes.name: handlerSetMemberRole
nodes.summary: Changes a member's role after a permission check, handling ownership transfer and notifying affected members over SSE.

nodes.name: CreateCalendarModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Animated modal for creating or editing a personal calendar, capturing name and color with validation and a random hint/color seeded on open.
nodes.languageNotes: React Native modal driven by useModalAnimation; edit vs create mode inferred from the optional calendar prop.

nodes.name: CreateCalendarModal
nodes.summary: Renders the create/edit calendar sheet: manages name/color/error state, seeds a random color and hint, validates input, then invokes onCreate or onEdit before closing.

nodes.name: SyncCalendarModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Animated multi-step modal for connecting an external calendar provider, offering Google social link and a CalDAV credential form.
nodes.languageNotes: Step-based wizard state; Google via authClient.linkSocial, CalDAV via useApi().connectCaldav.

nodes.name: SyncCalendarModal
nodes.summary: Renders the provider-connection wizard: steps between provider choice, Google linking, and CalDAV credential entry, handling loading and error states for each flow.

nodes.name: useSettingsStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store holding client-side user preferences (default calendar view, week start, accent color, time/date format, theme, notifications, onboarding flag, calendar order) with per-field setters and a loadSettings hydrator that overwrites state from the server.
nodes.languageNotes: Zustand create() factory typed with an explicit SettingsStore shape; loadSettings replaces the whole slice so server-fetched settings win over local defaults.

nodes.name: useSettingsStore
nodes.summary: Exported Zustand hook exposing user settings and their setters, with defaults (month view, monday start, 24h, dmy, system theme, kanji on) and a loadSettings action that replaces state from server-provided settings.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Central client-side API service exposing a useApi hook that wraps all backend calls (calendars, events, invites, members, settings, Google/CalDAV sync) through the authenticated fetch client.
nodes.languageNotes: Single React hook returns an object of async methods, each POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE going through authClient.$fetch with a shared throwOnError guard.

nodes.name: useApi
nodes.summary: React hook returning the full set of API methods (createCalendar, getEvents, createInvite, acceptInvite, setMemberRole, Google/CalDAV connection management, etc.) bound to the current server context.

nodes.name: AddEventModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Large modal component for creating and editing calendar events, handling the full event form: title, dates, all-day, calendars, notifications, recurrence (including an advanced RRULE builder), and a draggable docked sheet with gesture and keyboard animations.
nodes.languageNotes: React Native component using Reanimated shared values, gesture handling, and many useState/useEffect hooks; delegates recurrence and validation logic to lib/rrule.ts and lib/eventForm.ts.

nodes.name: AddEventModal
nodes.summary: Main functional component rendering the event form modal, wiring up state, animations, calendar selection, notifications, recurrence config, and the save flow.

nodes.name: eventForm.ts
nodes.summary: Pure validation helper for the event form, checking required fields (name, calendars, start/end ordering, URL) and returning per-field error messages used by AddEventModal.

nodes.name: validateEventForm
nodes.summary: Validates event form input and returns an object of field-specific error messages (empty when valid).

nodes.name: eventForm.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for validateEventForm covering the required-field and start/end ordering validation rules.

nodes.name: rrule.ts
nodes.summary: Recurrence-rule helpers converting between the UI's recurrence options and iCalendar RRULE strings: building rules, parsing simple/advanced rules, and producing human-readable descriptions.
nodes.languageNotes: Encapsulates RRULE FREQ/BYDAY/INTERVAL/COUNT syntax with regex parsing and day-name mapping tables.

nodes.name: buildRRule
nodes.summary: Builds an RRULE string from a recurrence option (or advanced config), deriving BYDAY from the start date for weekly rules.

nodes.name: parseRRule
nodes.summary: Parses a stored RRULE string back into the simple UI recurrence option when it matches a known simple pattern.

nodes.name: parseAdvanced
nodes.summary: Parses an RRULE string into an advanced recurrence config object (freq, interval, days, end type, count).

nodes.name: describeAdvanced
nodes.summary: Produces a human-readable summary string for an advanced recurrence config for display in the UI.

nodes.name: rrule.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the rrule helpers, verifying round-tripping of build/parse and the advanced-config parsing and description output.

nodes.name: calendars.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle data-access layer for calendars: CRUD on calendars plus membership, role, invite-token resolution, and cascading cleanup of orphaned events on calendar deletion.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Drizzle ORM query builder with relational findMany and conflict-handling inserts; removeCalendar computes orphaned events to avoid deleting events still linked to other calendars.

nodes.name: createCalendar
nodes.summary: Inserts a new calendar and registers the creating user as its first member.

nodes.name: getCalendarIDFromToken
nodes.summary: Resolves a calendar invite token to its target calendar ID.

nodes.name: getCalendar
nodes.summary: Fetches a single calendar record by its ID.

nodes.name: removeCalendar
nodes.summary: Deletes a calendar and cleans up events that become orphaned, leaving events still linked to other calendars intact.
nodes.languageNotes: Compares the calendar's event IDs against remaining calendar-event links to delete only genuinely orphaned events.

nodes.name: updateCalendar
nodes.summary: Updates a calendar record by ID and returns the updated row.

nodes.name: getUsersCalendars
nodes.summary: Returns all calendar memberships for a user, joined with the calendar records.

nodes.name: getCalendarMembers
nodes.summary: Returns all members of a calendar with their associated user records.

nodes.name: getCalendarEvents
nodes.summary: Returns all events linked to a calendar via the calendar-events join table.

nodes.name: getUserRoleForCalendar
nodes.summary: Looks up a user's role for a given calendar for authorization checks.

nodes.name: addCalendarMember
nodes.summary: Adds a user to a calendar as a read-only viewer, ignoring duplicates.

nodes.name: setMemberRole
nodes.summary: Updates a member's role on a calendar identified by user and calendar IDs.

nodes.name: removeCalendarMember
nodes.summary: Removes a user's membership from a calendar.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root manifest for the Musubi pnpm+turbo monorepo, declaring apps/* and packages/* workspaces plus turbo-driven dev/build scripts, drizzle db generate/migrate tasks, and a graph script that launches the understand-anything dashboard.
nodes.languageNotes: pnpm workspaces with Turborepo task orchestration; the graph script shells out to the locally cached understand-anything dashboard via npx vite.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the Expo client app, extending expo/tsconfig.base with strict mode, Node type declarations, and the @/* path alias mapping to the app root.
nodes.languageNotes: Extends the Expo base tsconfig and adds "types": ["node"] so Node globals resolve; @/* path alias maps imports to the app root.

nodes.name: external.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle ORM query layer for external calendar sync (Google/CalDAV): imports mirrored calendars, links/unlinks events, and upserts or deletes provider-sourced events with etag and cursor tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Drizzle transactions (db.transaction) for atomic multi-table writes and a raw sql`` fragment to cast accountID for the caldavAccounts leftJoin.

nodes.name: getUserExternalCalendars
nodes.summary: Returns a user's imported external calendars for a provider (optionally scoped to an account), joined with local calendar color.

nodes.name: importExternalCalendar
nodes.summary: Transactionally mirrors an external calendar into a new local calendar, records the external link, and adds the user as a member with the given role.

nodes.name: setAccountLabel
nodes.summary: Updates the display label on all external calendars belonging to a given provider account.

nodes.name: getExternalLinkForCalendar
nodes.summary: Resolves the external-sync link (provider, account, CalDAV server URL) for a local calendar via a leftJoin to caldavAccounts.

nodes.name: clearCalendarEvents
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes all events belonging to a calendar by stamping deletedAt on the events linked through calendarEvents.

nodes.name: linkEventToCalendars
nodes.summary: Inserts calendarEvents rows linking one event to multiple calendars and touches the event's updatedAt.

nodes.name: unlinkEventFromCalendars
nodes.summary: Removes an event's links to given calendars and deletes any orphaned externalEvents rows for those external calendars, then touches the event.

nodes.name: upsertExternalEvent
nodes.summary: Transactionally upserts a provider-sourced event: updates the existing mirrored event and etag, or inserts a new event with its calendar link and external mapping, setting the origin calendar to the mirror it was imported into.

nodes.name: deleteExternalEvent
nodes.summary: Hard-deletes the local event(s) mapped to a given external event ID for a provider and calendar.

nodes.name: getExternalEventID
nodes.summary: Looks up the provider-side external event ID for a local event within a given external calendar.

nodes.name: importExternalEvent
nodes.summary: Inserts an externalEvents mapping row recording the provider, external calendar/event IDs and etag for an already-created local event.

nodes.name: users.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle-based data access functions for the user domain: a dev-only bulk user reset plus upsert and read of per-user avatar blobs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Drizzle ORM query builder with onConflictDoUpdate for upsert semantics on the avatar table.

nodes.name: resetUsers
nodes.summary: Dev-only destructive helper that deletes all users, throwing ForbiddenError outside the dev environment.

nodes.name: setUserAvatar
nodes.summary: Upserts a user's avatar bytes and mime type into the userAvatars table, updating the timestamp on conflict.

nodes.name: getUserAvatar
nodes.summary: Fetches a single user's avatar row by user ID, returning null when none exists.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the @musubi/types package, re-exporting all shared type modules (errors, user, calendar, event, invite, settings, google, permissions).
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript barrel file using wildcard re-exports to expose the package's public type surface.

nodes.name: caldav.ts
nodes.summary: Express handlers for connecting, checking, and disconnecting CalDAV accounts. Validates credentials by fetching calendars before saving the app-password encrypted at rest.

nodes.name: handlerConnectCaldav
nodes.summary: Validates CalDAV credentials by creating a client and fetching calendars, then saves the account with the password encrypted.

nodes.name: handlerCheckCaldavStatus
nodes.summary: Returns the user's connected CalDAV accounts for the connections UI.

nodes.name: handlerDisconnectCaldav
nodes.summary: Removes the mirrored external calendars for a CalDAV account and deletes the stored account record.

nodes.name: connections.ts
nodes.summary: Handler for disconnecting an external provider account: removes mirrored calendars, then deletes CalDAV credentials or unlinks the OAuth account via Better Auth.

nodes.name: handlerDisconnectAccount
nodes.summary: Disconnects a provider account by removing its mirrored external calendars and deleting the CalDAV account or unlinking the OAuth account.

nodes.name: events.ts
nodes.summary: REST handlers for event CRUD plus link and fork operations across calendars. Enforces per-calendar permissions, pushes changes to external providers via the sync engine, and notifies members over SSE.

nodes.name: handlerCreateEvent
nodes.summary: Creates an event after a permission check, pushes it to connected external providers, and notifies calendar members.

nodes.name: handlerUpdateEvent
nodes.summary: Updates an event and diffs its calendar links, pushing creates, updates, and deletes to external providers for added, kept, and removed calendars.

nodes.name: handlerRemoveEvent
nodes.summary: Removes an event from the calendars the user can edit, unlinking or fully deleting depending on the event's origin, and syncs deletions to providers.

nodes.name: handlerLinkEvent
nodes.summary: Links an existing event into additional calendars the user can edit and mirrors it to any linked external providers.

nodes.name: handlerForkEvent
nodes.summary: Copies an event into another calendar as an independent event with a fresh UUID, pushing the copy to providers and notifying members.

nodes.name: handlerGetEvents
nodes.summary: Returns the user's events since a given timestamp, deduplicated across shared calendars, with deleted event IDs listed separately for client cache invalidation.

nodes.name: google.ts
nodes.summary: Handlers for the Google account connection: status check, OAuth token revocation against Google's endpoint, and triggering a provider sync to import Google calendars.

nodes.name: handlerCheckGoogleStatus
nodes.summary: Reports whether the user has a connected Google account.

nodes.name: handlerRevokeGoogle
nodes.summary: Revokes the stored Google refresh token against Google's OAuth revoke endpoint and cleans the user's saved tokens.

nodes.name: handlerGetGoogleCalendars
nodes.summary: Runs a full provider sync for the user, importing Google calendars and their events.

nodes.name: invites.ts
nodes.summary: Handler that creates a share invite token for a calendar after verifying the requester has invite permission.

nodes.name: handlerCreateCalendarInvite
nodes.summary: Validates the invite payload and creates an invite token for a calendar, guarded by an invite permission check.

nodes.name: server.ts
nodes.summary: Public health and server-info endpoints used by clients to verify the API is reachable.

nodes.name: handlerServerStatus
nodes.summary: Health check endpoint returning a 200 JSON response.

nodes.name: handlerServer
nodes.summary: Returns basic server identification info as JSON.

nodes.name: settings.ts
nodes.summary: Handlers for reading and saving per-user settings, validated against SettingsSchema.

nodes.name: handlerGetSettings
nodes.summary: Returns the requesting user's saved settings.

nodes.name: handlerSaveSettings
nodes.summary: Validates the settings payload with SettingsSchema and persists it for the user.

nodes.name: stream.ts
nodes.summary: Server-sent events infrastructure: keeps a map of connected clients and exposes notifyCalendarMembers, used by other handlers to push realtime calendar and event updates.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a module-level Map of userID to SSE response as an in-process client registry — one API instance only.

nodes.name: handlerStream
nodes.summary: Opens a server-sent-events connection, registers the client in the connection map, and cleans it up on close.

nodes.name: notifyCalendarMembers
nodes.summary: Writes a typed JSON payload to each connected member's SSE stream, skipping members without an open connection.

nodes.name: users.ts
nodes.summary: User account handlers: account deletion via Better Auth, password reset entry, and avatar upload/download with MIME sniffing of the uploaded image bytes.

nodes.name: handlerDeleteUser
nodes.summary: Deletes the requesting user's account through the Better Auth API.

nodes.name: handlerResetUsers
nodes.summary: Public password reset entry point that triggers the reset flow for a user.

nodes.name: handlerUploadAvatar
nodes.summary: Accepts a base64 avatar image, sniffs its MIME type from the raw bytes, and stores it for the user.

nodes.name: handlerGetAvatar
nodes.summary: Serves a user's avatar image publicly with the stored content type, since image tags cannot send auth headers.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Express server entry point: configures CORS, JSON parsing, and logging middleware, mounts Better Auth under /api/auth, registers all /api/v1 routes with async error wrapping, and runs an hourly cleanup job for expired invites, sessions, and event tombstones.
nodes.languageNotes: Routes are wrapped with a small `wrap` helper so rejected handler promises reach the error middleware instead of crashing the process.

nodes.name: error_handler.ts
nodes.summary: Central Express error middleware that maps thrown errors (including Zod validation failures) to appropriate HTTP status codes and JSON error bodies, logging unexpected errors.

nodes.name: middlewareErrorHandler
nodes.summary: Classifies thrown errors by type and responds with the matching HTTP status and a JSON error message.

nodes.name: log_handler.ts
nodes.summary: Request logging middleware that prints method, path, status, and duration when each response finishes.

nodes.name: middlewareLogHandler
nodes.summary: Logs each request with its response status and elapsed time via a finish listener on the response.

nodes.name: require_auth.ts
nodes.summary: Authentication middleware that resolves the Better Auth session from request headers and rejects unauthenticated requests before protected route handlers run.

nodes.name: requireAuth
nodes.summary: Resolves the Better Auth session and calls next only for authenticated requests, making the session available to downstream handlers.

nodes.name: permissions.ts
nodes.summary: Role-based permission helpers for calendar actions: assertCan throws on missing rights, canDo returns a boolean, and assertCanEditEvent resolves an event's origin calendar to check edit rights.

nodes.name: assertCan
nodes.summary: Looks up the user's role for a calendar and throws if the role does not permit the requested action.

nodes.name: canDo
nodes.summary: Non-throwing variant of the permission check: returns whether the user's calendar role permits the action.

nodes.name: assertCanEditEvent
nodes.summary: Verifies the user may edit an event by checking its origin calendar, falling back to any linked calendar the user can edit.

nodes.name: adapter.ts
nodes.summary: Provider-agnostic contract for external calendar sync: NormalizedEvent, ExternalCalendarInfo, and FetchChangesResult types plus the CalendarAdapter interface every provider adapter implements.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure type module — the adapter pattern keeps the sync engine independent of Google/CalDAV specifics; the engine never talks to a provider API directly.

nodes.name: caldav.ts
nodes.summary: CalDAV implementation of the CalendarAdapter contract: converts between iCalendar (via ICAL.js) and NormalizedEvent, decrypts stored credentials, and handles etag-based change detection and push operations.
nodes.languageNotes: All-day events are normalized to UTC-midnight anchors when translating between iCalendar date values and JS Dates.

nodes.name: icalToNormalized
nodes.summary: Parses an iCalendar object into a NormalizedEvent, handling all-day date anchoring, recurrence rules, and cancelled status.

nodes.name: toIcal
nodes.summary: Serializes a Musubi event into an iCalendar VEVENT, mapping all-day events to date-only values for CalDAV push.

nodes.name: google.ts
nodes.summary: Google Calendar implementation of the CalendarAdapter contract using the Calendar v3 REST API: fetches access tokens via Better Auth, supports incremental sync tokens, and translates events and recurrence rules between Google's format and NormalizedEvent.
nodes.languageNotes: Timezone conversion uses an Intl-based single-iteration approximation instead of a timezone library — deliberately accepted to be up to 1h off around DST transitions.

nodes.name: zonedToUtc
nodes.summary: Converts a wall-clock time in a named timezone to a UTC Date using Intl.DateTimeFormat, avoiding a timezone library dependency.

nodes.name: sanitizeRecurrence
nodes.summary: Normalizes Google's recurrence lines into RRULE text the app's rrule expansion handles: converts TZID EXDATEs to UTC and anchors BYMONTH on yearly rules.

nodes.name: toNormalized
nodes.summary: Maps a Google Calendar event JSON item to a NormalizedEvent, including cancelled tombstones, all-day handling, and sanitized recurrence.

nodes.name: toGoogleRecurrence
nodes.summary: Converts the app's RRULE text into Google's recurrence array format for pushes.

nodes.name: toGoogleEvent
nodes.summary: Maps a Musubi event to the Google Calendar event JSON body, choosing date vs dateTime fields for all-day events.

nodes.name: caldav_client.ts
nodes.summary: Thin factory that creates a configured tsdav DAVClient for a CalDAV server URL and basic-auth credentials.

nodes.name: createCaldavClient
nodes.summary: Creates a DAV client instance configured with server URL and basic-auth username/password for CalDAV operations.

nodes.name: crypto.ts
nodes.summary: AES-256-GCM encryption helpers for storing CalDAV app-passwords at rest, keyed by the CALDAV_ENC_KEY config value with a fresh IV per encryption.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses authenticated GCM mode so the auth tag detects ciphertext tampering on decrypt; ciphertext is stored as iv:tag:data in base64.

nodes.name: encryptSecret
nodes.summary: Encrypts a plaintext secret with AES-256-GCM using a random 96-bit IV and returns iv:tag:ciphertext in base64.

nodes.name: decryptSecret
nodes.summary: Decrypts an iv:tag:ciphertext blob with AES-256-GCM, verifying the auth tag to detect tampering.

nodes.name: engine.ts
nodes.summary: Generic multi-provider sync engine: registers Google and CalDAV adapters, reconciles remote calendar lists with local mirrors, pulls incremental event changes per calendar cursor, and pushes local event creates/updates/deletes out to linked providers.
nodes.languageNotes: New providers are added by registering an adapter in one record — the engine only ever talks to providers through the CalendarAdapter interface.

nodes.name: getAdapter
nodes.summary: Looks up the registered CalendarAdapter for a provider name, or null if unregistered.

nodes.name: toEventValues
nodes.summary: Maps a NormalizedEvent to the primitive column values the DB layer expects, defaulting organizer and taking color from the calendar.

nodes.name: syncProvider
nodes.summary: Full pull sync for one connected account: reconciles the remote calendar list with local mirrors (import new, drop removed, refresh read-only roles), then applies each calendar's incremental event changes and persists the new cursor.

nodes.name: syncUser
nodes.summary: Syncs every connected account of every registered provider for a user, isolating per-provider failures so one broken provider does not block the rest.

nodes.name: pushEventToProviders
nodes.summary: Pushes an event create/update/delete to every provider-linked calendar the event belongs to; for deletes the caller must invoke it before removing the local event.

nodes.name: pushEventToCalendars
nodes.summary: Pushes an event action to a specific set of calendars, resolving each calendar's external link and adapter, recording new external event IDs on create, and logging per-calendar failures without aborting.

nodes.name: sign-in.tsx
nodes.summary: Email/password sign-in screen with field-level validation, Better Auth email sign-in, and a password-reset request modal.

nodes.name: SignIn
nodes.summary: Sign-in screen component that validates email/password, calls authClient.signIn.email, and routes to the main tabs on success.

nodes.name: sign-up.tsx
nodes.summary: Registration screen collecting name, email, and password with per-field validation, calling Better Auth signUp and routing into the app on success.

nodes.name: SignUp
nodes.summary: Sign-up screen component chaining focus across name/email/password inputs, validating fields, and calling authClient.signUp.email.

nodes.name: welcome.tsx
nodes.summary: Auth landing screen offering native Google Sign-In, navigation to email sign-in/sign-up, and a custom self-hosted server URL modal with a connectivity test.

nodes.name: Welcome
nodes.summary: Welcome screen component handling native Google Sign-In via idToken with Better Auth social sign-in, plus server URL switching validated against the API health endpoint.

nodes.name: calendars.tsx
nodes.summary: Calendars tab screen grouping native and externally synced calendars by provider account, with drag-reorder persistence to server settings, create/sync/detail modals, and account disconnect.

nodes.name: CalendarsTab
nodes.summary: Tab component that memoizes calendar groups sorted by user-defined order, counts events per calendar, persists reorder via api.saveSettings, and orchestrates create/sync/detail modals.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: Root route auth gate that reads the Better Auth session and redirects to the tabs or the welcome flow.

nodes.name: Index
nodes.summary: Tiny redirect component using useServer and authClient.useSession to decide the initial route.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: First onboarding step where the user sets a display name and uploads an avatar before continuing to the next onboarding screen.

nodes.name: OnboardingProfile
nodes.summary: Profile-setup component that picks and uploads an avatar (api.uploadAvatar + authClient.updateUser) and saves the display name before routing onward.

nodes.name: TextInputModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable animated single-text-input modal with optional async validation via onTest, error display, and haptic feedback; used for server URL entry and settings edits.

nodes.name: InputModal
nodes.summary: Modal component that runs an optional onTest validator before confirming input, showing an error and warn haptic on failure.

nodes.name: CalendarFilterBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Horizontal filter chip bar toggling per-calendar visibility with a long-press solo mode, optimistic local display state, haptics, and ordering from user settings.

nodes.name: CalendarFilterBar
nodes.summary: Filter bar component updating a local display set immediately and deferring onToggle/onSolo callbacks to the next animation frame for responsiveness.

nodes.name: ReorderableCalendarList.tsx
nodes.summary: Gesture-driven list rendering calendar groups with long-press drag reordering of both individual calendar rows and whole account sections, animated with Reanimated shared values.
nodes.languageNotes: Positions items absolutely from a computed layout map instead of using a list library, driving drag offsets through Reanimated shared values and runOnJS callbacks.

nodes.name: ReorderableCalendarList
nodes.summary: Main list component computing item layout tops, tracking drag state for rows and account groups, and committing reorders via the onReorder callback with the flattened calendar id order.

nodes.name: PositionedItem
nodes.summary: Absolutely positioned animated wrapper that registers its shared Y value in a registry, animates to its base position, and follows the drag offset when dragged.

nodes.name: SectionHeader
nodes.summary: Account section header with a long-press pan gesture that lifts the whole group and reports drag start/move/end back to the list via runOnJS.

nodes.name: Btn.tsx
nodes.summary: Button UI primitive built on Tap supporting primary/secondary variants, an optional icon, and loading/disabled states; the app-wide standard button.

nodes.name: Btn
nodes.summary: Button component rendering label, optional icon, and an activity indicator when loading, styled per variant from the theme.

nodes.name: Tap.tsx
nodes.summary: Pressable wrapper primitive adding tap haptic feedback and press styling; the base touch interaction component used across the app.

nodes.name: Tap
nodes.summary: Pressable component that fires the tap haptic on press and applies pressed-state styling before delegating to onPress.

nodes.name: calendar_hints.ts
nodes.summary: Static CALENDAR_HINTS array of playful calendar-name suggestions used as randomized placeholder text in the create-calendar modal.

nodes.name: ServerContext.tsx
nodes.summary: React context providing the selected API server URL and a matching Better Auth client instance, loaded from and persisted to SecureStore for self-hosted server switching.

nodes.name: ServerProvider
nodes.summary: Provider component that reads the stored API URL on mount, recreates the auth client via createClient when the URL changes, and exposes setNewServerUrl to persist a new server.

nodes.name: useServer
nodes.summary: Hook returning the server context (apiUrl, authClient, setNewServerUrl); the standard access point for the auth client throughout the app.

nodes.name: avatar.ts
nodes.summary: Helper that opens the device image library and returns a resized, base64-encoded avatar image via expo-image-manipulator.

nodes.name: pickAvatarBase64
nodes.summary: Launches the image picker, resizes the chosen image, and returns it as a base64 string for avatar upload.

nodes.name: confirm.ts
nodes.summary: Native confirmation helpers using ActionSheetIOS on iOS and Alert elsewhere: a destructive confirm dialog and a multi-option chooser, both with warn haptics.

nodes.name: chooseOption
nodes.summary: Presents a list of options as an iOS action sheet or Android alert, invoking the selected option's onPress handler.

nodes.name: confirm
nodes.summary: Shows a destructive confirmation prompt (action sheet or alert) and calls onConfirm when the user accepts.

nodes.name: haptics.ts
nodes.summary: Thin wrappers around expo-haptics exposing semantic feedback helpers (tap, select, thump, success, warn) used throughout the client UI.

nodes.name: tap
nodes.summary: Light impact haptic for ordinary taps.

nodes.name: thump
nodes.summary: Heavier impact haptic for significant interactions like drag grabs and solo toggles.

nodes.name: success
nodes.summary: Success notification haptic fired after completed actions such as sign-in or calendar creation.

nodes.name: warn
nodes.summary: Warning notification haptic fired on validation failures and errors.

nodes.name: auth-client.ts
nodes.summary: Factory creating a Better Auth client configured with the Expo plugin for a given server URL, enabling per-server auth in the self-hosting flow.

nodes.name: createClient
nodes.summary: Builds a Better Auth client (createAuthClient + expoClient plugin) pointed at the supplied API URL.

nodes.name: agenda.tsx
nodes.summary: Agenda tab screen listing upcoming events grouped by day, with pull-to-refresh, lazy 'show more' pagination, and inline event detail / add-event modals.
nodes.languageNotes: Expo Router tab screen; filters all-day events by calendar day rather than UTC instant to avoid hiding them mid-morning.

nodes.name: AgendaTab
nodes.summary: Default-exported screen component that reads events/calendars/settings stores, groups future visible events by date key, and renders the scrollable agenda with detail and creation modals.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: Main calendar tab orchestrating the custom month/week/day views: month-to-day drill zoom, drag-to-create drafts, docked event composer, recurring-event expansion, and optimistic event moves with API persistence.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of react-native-reanimated shared values and interpolated styles for the month-to-day zoom transition; Android back button closes the drill via useFocusEffect.

nodes.name: MainTab
nodes.summary: Default-exported screen component managing calendar mode state, drill-down animation, draft creation, import-store prefills, and optimistic event move persistence via the API with haptic/toast feedback on failure.

nodes.name: ModeSwitch.tsx
nodes.summary: Dropdown view switcher for the calendar: a custom trigger opens a small animated paper card offering Day/Week/Month modes with a checkmark on the current one.

nodes.name: ModeSwitch
nodes.summary: Render-prop component: receives a trigger renderer and an onChange callback, toggles an absolutely-positioned mode card with fade animations.

nodes.name: MonthView.tsx
nodes.summary: Infinite-paging month grid for the custom calendar: renders day cells with timed-event chips and spanning all-day bars, reporting the tapped cell's rect to drive the month-to-day zoom.

nodes.name: MonthView
nodes.summary: Paged month grid component: buckets events by day, computes all-day lane spans per week row, caps visible chips per cell, and emits onDayPress with the cell rect for the drill animation.

nodes.name: TimelineView.tsx
nodes.summary: Day/week timeline of the custom calendar: minutes-to-pixels layout driven by a shared hour-height value (pinch zoom), layered gestures for drag-to-create ghosts, event hold-to-move, and a live now-indicator, paged via InfinitePager.
nodes.languageNotes: All vertical geometry derives from a reanimated shared value so pinch zoom rescales on the UI thread without re-render; event positions live in shared values for flicker-free drag-and-drop.

nodes.name: TimelineView
nodes.summary: Exported wrapper that buckets events by day, computes page start dates for day or week mode, and hosts the InfinitePager rendering one TimelinePage per visible page.

nodes.name: TimelinePage
nodes.summary: Single timeline page rendering hour grid, all-day strip, overlapping event segments, and the draft ghost; implements tap-to-create, long-press drag-to-create, ghost resize/move gestures, and auto-scroll to now.

nodes.name: HourLine
nodes.summary: Presentational row drawing one hour gridline and its gutter time label at the position derived from the shared hour height.

nodes.name: TimelineEventBlock
nodes.summary: Positioned event block on the timeline: tap opens detail, hold lifts and drags to move across days/minutes with snapping; keeps its position in shared values for a flicker-free drop.

nodes.name: layout.ts
nodes.summary: Shared geometry constants, gesture-tuning values, and date math for the custom calendar views: minute/pixel conversions, day keys, event day-bucketing, month grids, overlapping-segment layout, and all-day span lanes.
nodes.languageNotes: All tuning magic numbers (snap minutes, hold delays, zoom bounds) are named constants with comments — the file doubles as the calendar's tuning panel. All-day events are keyed in UTC, timed ones locally.

nodes.name: startOfWeek
nodes.summary: Returns midnight of the week's first day for a date, respecting the user's Sunday/Monday week-start preference.

nodes.name: eventDayKeys
nodes.summary: Lists every YYYY-MM-DD key an event touches, treating the end as exclusive for timed events so midnight-ending events don't bleed into the next day.

nodes.name: bucketByDay
nodes.summary: Builds a Map from day key to the events occurring on that day, the shared lookup structure behind both month and timeline views.

nodes.name: monthGrid
nodes.summary: Produces the 6-week grid of Date cells for a month page, aligned to the configured week start.

nodes.name: daySegments
nodes.summary: Computes side-by-side column layout for overlapping timed events within a day, returning start/end minutes plus column index and count per event.

nodes.name: allDaySpans
nodes.summary: Assigns multi-day all-day events to horizontal lanes across a row of days, producing the spanning bars shown in month and timeline headers.

nodes.name: CalendarDetailModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-screen modal showing a single calendar's own month/week/day views — a scoped mini calendar with its own mode switch, drill zoom, event detail, composer, and calendar settings/edit entry points.

nodes.name: CalendarDetail
nodes.summary: Default-exported modal component that filters store events to the selected calendar, expands recurrences, reuses MonthView/TimelineView with the drill-zoom animation, and gates editing by calendar role.

nodes.name: CalendarHeader.tsx
nodes.summary: Header bar for the main calendar tab: kanji month name with year, ModeSwitch dropdown trigger, today button, and a refresh control with loading spinner.

nodes.name: CalendarHeader
nodes.summary: Presentational header component wiring anchor date, calendar mode, today-press, and refresh callbacks into the kanji month display and controls.

nodes.name: Empty.tsx
nodes.summary: Zen empty-state primitive: a single large kanji character above one quiet line of text, used wherever a list has nothing to show.

nodes.name: Empty
nodes.summary: Stateless component rendering a customizable kanji (default 空) and a caption in the app's muted palette.

nodes.name: colors.ts
nodes.summary: Named calendar color palette (dune, shu, moss, ochre, indigo) offered when creating or recoloring calendars.

nodes.name: const.ts
nodes.summary: Static date-label constants: Japanese kanji month names (一月 through 十二月) used in calendar headers, and English weekday names.

nodes.name: event_hints.ts
nodes.summary: A long list of humorous placeholder event titles ('Another Meeting That Could've Been an Email') shown randomly as the title hint in the event composer.

nodes.name: useVisibleEvents.ts
nodes.summary: Hook memoizing the events visible under the current calendar filter set, sorted by start time.

nodes.name: useVisibleEvents
nodes.summary: Filters events to those linked to any active calendar and sorts them by start, memoized on events and the active-calendar set.

nodes.name: calendarOrder.ts
nodes.summary: Single flattened calendar ordering used everywhere calendars line up: Musubi group first, personal default pinned on top, then the user's saved drag order with stable fallback.

nodes.name: sortCalendars
nodes.summary: Generic stable sort placing non-provider (Musubi) calendars first, the default calendar on top, then following the saved order array; unlisted calendars keep their natural position.

nodes.name: datetimeFormat.ts
nodes.summary: Central date/time string formatting driven by the user's 12h/24h and dmy/mdy/ymd settings, so every screen renders dates the same way; backed by dayjs.

nodes.name: formatTime
nodes.summary: Formats a Date as '14:30' or '2:30 PM' according to the user's time-format setting.

nodes.name: formatDateMedium
nodes.summary: Medium date form for chips and rows (e.g. '31 Dec 2026') ordered per the user's date-format preference.

nodes.name: formatDateLong
nodes.summary: Long descriptive date with weekday (e.g. 'Wednesday, 31 December') ordered to match the date-format preference.

nodes.name: eventColor.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves an event's display color from its origin calendar live, so recoloring a calendar recolors its events; falls back to the first linked calendar, then a neutral theme color.

nodes.name: eventColor
nodes.summary: Looks up the origin calendar's color in a Map, falling back to the first linked calendar and finally the neutral fg4 theme color.

nodes.name: eventPermissions.ts
nodes.summary: Client-side mirror of the server's event edit rule: the origin calendar's role governs edits, or with no origin, any linked calendar the user can edit in counts.

nodes.name: canEditEvent
nodes.summary: Returns whether the user may edit an event, checking the origin calendar's role via the shared can() helper from @musubi/types, matching the server's assertCanEditEvent.

nodes.name: liveEvent.ts
nodes.summary: Keeps an open event-detail modal in sync with SSE store updates by reading the live store row, while preserving the tapped occurrence's start/end for recurring events.

nodes.name: liveEventDetail
nodes.summary: Finds the live store row for the detail snapshot; for recurring events merges the live row with the tapped occurrence's start/end since the store carries the series master's times.

nodes.name: _layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Expo Router layout for the auth route group, rendering a themed Stack navigator for sign-in/sign-up screens.

nodes.name: AuthLayout
nodes.summary: Default-exported layout component that wraps auth screens in a Stack navigator with theme styling.

nodes.name: _layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root Expo Router layout that bootstraps the app: runs SQLite migrations, loads fonts, checks minimum client version against the server, handles .ics deep links, applies theme, and registers push notifications.
nodes.languageNotes: Splits bootstrap into three nested components (RootLayout > providers > AppLoader > AppContent) so hooks that depend on providers mount in the right order.

nodes.name: AppContent
nodes.summary: Core bootstrap component: runs drizzle migrations, checks server minClientVersion via semver, guards auth routing, hides the splash screen, and wires the incoming-URL listener for .ics imports.

nodes.name: AppLoader
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective color scheme from settings and system preference, applies the theme, and sets the system UI background color before rendering AppContent.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Default-exported root component that registers push notifications on mount and composes the provider tree (ServerProvider etc.) around AppLoader.

nodes.name: _layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Expo Router layout for the onboarding flow, providing a themed Stack navigator for onboarding screens.

nodes.name: OnboardingLayout
nodes.summary: Default-exported layout component wrapping onboarding screens in a themed Stack navigator.

nodes.name: LoadingOverlay.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-screen loading overlay that fades out with a Reanimated timing animation and unmounts itself once the app signals ready.

nodes.name: LoadingOverlay
nodes.summary: Animated overlay component that fades opacity to zero when `ready` flips true, then unmounts via runOnJS.

nodes.name: SettingRow.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable settings-screen row components: a labeled toggle switch and a segmented option picker, both built on the Tap primitive.

nodes.name: SettingRowToggle
nodes.summary: Settings row rendering a label with a boolean toggle switch and change callback.

nodes.name: SettingRowOptions
nodes.summary: Settings row rendering a segmented control over a list of options with optional display labels and a change callback.

nodes.name: UpdateRequiredModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Blocking full-screen modal shown when the client version is below the server's minimum, displaying current vs required versions with a button that opens the app store.

nodes.name: UpdateRequiredModal
nodes.summary: Modal component showing an update-required message and an Update button that opens the store URL via Linking.

nodes.name: CalendarSkeleton.tsx
nodes.summary: Placeholder skeleton for the calendar screen: a pulsing 7-column month grid animated with Reanimated repeat/sequence timing.

nodes.name: CalendarSkeleton
nodes.summary: Renders a pulsing grid of weekday headers and 5 rows of 7 day cells as a calendar loading placeholder.

nodes.name: Toast.tsx
nodes.summary: Imperative toast system: a zustand-backed showToast() function callable from anywhere, plus a ToastHost component that animates the toast in/out with optional action button and auto-dismiss.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a module-level zustand store so toasts can be triggered outside React via useToastStore.getState().

nodes.name: showToast
nodes.summary: Imperative helper that pushes a toast into the zustand store from any code, React or not.

nodes.name: ToastHost
nodes.summary: Host component mounted once at the root that renders the active toast with slide/fade animations, safe-area placement, auto-dismiss timer, and optional action tap.

nodes.name: theme.ts
nodes.summary: Central theme module for the client: font definitions, light/dark color palettes, the shared StyleSheet built by makeStyles, calendar-specific theme values, and applyTheme() which mutates the exported objects in place when the scheme changes.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports mutable singletons (colors, styles, calendarTheme) that applyTheme() rewrites via Object.assign, so all consumers see scheme changes without re-importing.

nodes.name: applyTheme
nodes.summary: Switches the active color scheme by Object.assign-ing the chosen palette, rebuilt styles, and calendar theme into the exported singleton objects.

nodes.name: makeStyles
nodes.summary: Builds the app-wide StyleSheet from the currently active color palette, covering layout, typography, buttons, modals, and settings styles.

nodes.name: makeCalendarTheme
nodes.summary: Builds the calendar-component theme object (day cells, today highlight, event colors) from the active palette.

nodes.name: url.ts
nodes.summary: Two constants: the default self-hosted server URL (https://musubi.frgtn.dev) and the API version segment (v1) used to build request paths.

nodes.name: migrations.js
nodes.summary: Drizzle-generated migrations bundle for Expo SQLite: imports the migration journal and four SQL migration files and exports them for useMigrations at app startup.

nodes.name: ics.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the ICS parser, verifying event extraction from raw iCalendar text.

nodes.name: ics.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal hand-rolled iCalendar (.ics) parser: unfolds folded lines, unescapes text, parses DATE/DATE-TIME values, and extracts VEVENT blocks into draft event objects for import.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements RFC 5545 line unfolding and text unescaping directly instead of pulling in an ICS library.

nodes.name: parseICS
nodes.summary: Parses raw .ics text into event drafts by scanning VEVENT blocks for SUMMARY, DESCRIPTION, LOCATION, DTSTART, and DTEND, skipping events with invalid dates.

nodes.name: useImportStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store bridging deep-linked .ics files to the calendar screen: the root layout stores a pending ICSDraft, and the calendar picks it up to prefill the event composer.

nodes.name: _layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Expo Router tab layout that gates the main app: hydrates calendars and events from the local SQLite cache, kicks off a server refresh, connects the live SSE event stream, and redirects users who have not finished onboarding back into the onboarding flow.

nodes.name: TabLayout
nodes.summary: Default-exported tab navigator component that loads cached data before first paint, triggers a background refresh, subscribes to the event stream, and enforces the onboarding redirect.

nodes.name: settings.tsx
nodes.summary: Settings tab screen covering profile edits (avatar, display name), theme and calendar preferences persisted through the API, and sign-out / account-deletion flows that clear the local cache, scheduled notifications, and the Google session.

nodes.name: SettingsTab
nodes.summary: Settings screen component with handlers for avatar upload, name change, per-preference saves via api.saveSettings, pull-to-refresh, sign-out, and destructive account deletion.

nodes.name: sync.tsx
nodes.summary: Final onboarding step where users connect external calendar accounts (deduplicated per provider) via the sync modal, then finish onboarding by persisting settings to the server and navigating into the main app.

nodes.name: OnboardingSync
nodes.summary: Onboarding sync screen component that refreshes data on focus, groups connected accounts by provider flavor, and finishes onboarding by saving settings and marking the user as onboarded.

nodes.name: OnboardingScaffold.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared layout wrapper for onboarding screens rendering step-progress dots, a kanji accent, title/subtitle, content, and an actions slot, while recording the current route so the flow can resume after OAuth deep-link re-navigation.

nodes.name: OnboardingScaffold
nodes.summary: Presentational scaffold component taking step, kanji, title, subtitle, actions, and children props; side-effect records the current pathname as the last onboarding route.

nodes.name: schema.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle SQLite schema for the client-side cache: scheduled notification records (with the user's reminder offset), a full local mirror of events, and a key/value sync-meta table for delta-sync bookkeeping.
nodes.languageNotes: Drizzle ORM schema over expo-sqlite; dates stored as ISO text, booleans as 0/1 ints, and array fields as JSON strings.

nodes.name: useEventsStream.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that opens an authenticated Server-Sent Events connection to the API and applies live event and calendar create/update/delete messages to the Zustand stores in real time.

nodes.name: useConnectToEventStream
nodes.summary: Hook that fetches the auth session, subscribes to the SSE endpoint, dispatches parsed messages to local store mutations (add/update/remove event, update/remove calendar), and closes the stream on unmount.

nodes.name: useRefreshData.ts
nodes.summary: Hook returning a refresh() routine that delta-syncs events since the last sync timestamp, repairs event-to-calendar membership, reconciles the SQLite cache and notification schedules, and reloads settings, calendars, and events into the stores.

nodes.name: useRefreshData
nodes.summary: Returns an async refresh function performing either a full or delta event fetch based on last-sync, upserting/deleting cached rows, dropping events whose calendars the user no longer belongs to, and reconciling notification schedules.

nodes.name: onboardingState.ts
nodes.summary: Module-level holder of the last visited onboarding route so the flow survives the OAuth deep-link re-navigation instead of resetting to step 1; resets naturally on full app restart.

nodes.name: setOnboardingRoute
nodes.summary: Records the given route string as the last visited onboarding route.

nodes.name: getOnboardingRoute
nodes.summary: Returns the last recorded onboarding route (defaults to /onboarding).

nodes.name: db.ts
nodes.summary: Opens the expo-sqlite database synchronously and exports the shared Drizzle client instance used by all local persistence services.

nodes.name: eventsCache.ts
nodes.summary: SQLite-backed offline cache for events and calendars: row mapping, validated upsert/replace/delete of event rows in chunks, calendar list persistence as JSON in the sync-meta table, and last-sync timestamp bookkeeping.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Drizzle ORM query builder with onConflictDoUpdate upserts against the key/value sync_meta table.

nodes.name: toRow
nodes.summary: Maps an Event domain object to a SQLite row, normalizing dates to ISO strings and serializing calendar IDs to JSON.

nodes.name: fromRow
nodes.summary: Maps a cached SQLite row back to an Event domain object, parsing the JSON calendar list.

nodes.name: cacheGetAllEvents
nodes.summary: Reads all cached event rows and returns them as Event objects.

nodes.name: cacheUpsertEvents
nodes.summary: Delete-then-insert upsert of events into the cache, filtering out events with invalid dates and inserting in chunks.

nodes.name: cacheDeleteEvents
nodes.summary: Deletes cached event rows by ID list.

nodes.name: cacheReplaceAllEvents
nodes.summary: Wipes the events table and re-inserts the given (date-validated) events, used for full syncs.

nodes.name: cacheSetCalendars
nodes.summary: Persists the calendar list as a JSON string under the calendars key in the sync-meta table via upsert.

nodes.name: cacheGetCalendars
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the cached calendar list from the sync-meta table.

nodes.name: getLastSync
nodes.summary: Returns the stored last-sync timestamp from the sync-meta table, if any.

nodes.name: setLastSync
nodes.summary: Validates and upserts the last-sync ISO timestamp into the sync-meta table.

nodes.name: cacheClearAll
nodes.summary: Clears the entire local cache (events and sync-meta), used on sign-out and account deletion.

nodes.name: notifications.ts
nodes.summary: Local event-reminder service: computes the next notification trigger (including recurring-event occurrences), schedules and cancels Expo notifications backed by a SQLite table, reconciles schedules against synced events, and handles Android channel setup plus permission registration.

nodes.name: nextTrigger
nodes.summary: Computes the next future trigger time for an event's reminder, expanding recurring events to find the next occurrence whose reminder is still in the future.

nodes.name: getEventNotification
nodes.summary: Returns the stored notification row for an event, if one is scheduled.

nodes.name: upsertEventNotification
nodes.summary: Cancels any existing scheduled notification for the event, computes the next trigger, and either schedules a replacement (updating or inserting the DB row) or removes the row when no future trigger exists.

nodes.name: cancelEventNotification
nodes.summary: Cancels the OS-scheduled notification for an event and deletes its tracking row.

nodes.name: syncEventNotification
nodes.summary: Reschedules an event's notification with its original offset after the event changes, if a reminder exists.

nodes.name: reconcileEventNotifications
nodes.summary: Walks all stored notification rows after a sync, cancelling and deleting reminders for events that no longer exist and rescheduling the rest with their saved offsets.

nodes.name: clearAllEventNotifications
nodes.summary: Cancels every scheduled notification and empties the notifications table, used on sign-out.

nodes.name: registerForPushNotificationsAsync
nodes.summary: Sets up the Android notification channel and requests notification permissions, returning whether they were granted.

nodes.name: useEventsStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store holding all events with local (optimistic) mutations plus API-backed add/update/remove/link/fork actions that keep the SQLite cache and notification schedules in sync with each change.

nodes.name: [token].tsx
nodes.summary: Expo Router deep-link screen for calendar invites: fetches the calendar preview from an invite token, shows members and upcoming events, and lets the user accept or decline the invitation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an Expo Router dynamic route segment ([token]) to receive the invite token via useLocalSearchParams.

nodes.name: Invite
nodes.summary: Screen component that loads calendar data for an invite token, redirects existing members, and handles accepting the invite with haptic feedback and store refresh.

nodes.name: calendar.tsx
nodes.summary: Onboarding step where the user names and picks a color for their default personal calendar, persisting changes via the API and local store before advancing.

nodes.name: OnboardingCalendar
nodes.summary: Onboarding screen component that edits the default calendar's name and color, fires an optimistic local update, and navigates to the next onboarding step.

nodes.name: Avatar.tsx
nodes.summary: Small reusable avatar component that renders a user image or falls back to the uppercased first letter of the name.

nodes.name: Avatar
nodes.summary: Renders a circular avatar of configurable size, showing the user's image when available or an initial-letter placeholder otherwise.

nodes.name: CalendarPickerModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Bottom-sheet modal listing the user's editable calendars for selection, with an optional filter predicate and empty-state label; used for linking/forking events across calendars.

nodes.name: CalendarPickerModal
nodes.summary: Animated modal component that filters calendars by edit permission plus a caller-supplied predicate, invokes onSelect for the tapped calendar, and closes with the shared modal animation.

nodes.name: CalendarSettingsModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Per-calendar settings bottom sheet offering invite-link sharing, member role management, edit, delete, and leave actions, gated by the current user's permissions.

nodes.name: CalendarSettingsModal
nodes.summary: Modal component that creates and shares invite links via the native share sheet, opens the member roles modal, and handles leave/delete/edit flows based on the user's role capabilities.

nodes.name: EventDetailModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Event detail bottom sheet showing time, duration, location (with maps deep-link), and calendar memberships; supports editing, linking/forking/unlinking events across calendars, and deleting single occurrences, following occurrences, or whole recurring series.
nodes.languageNotes: Recurring-event deletion is implemented client-side by mutating the master event's RRULE (exclude occurrence / end series before date) rather than deleting rows.

nodes.name: EventDetailModal
nodes.summary: Large modal component rendering full event details with permission-aware actions: edit, link/fork/unlink to other calendars via the calendar picker, and three recurrence-aware delete strategies that keep notifications in sync.

nodes.name: MemberRolesModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Member management bottom sheet listing calendar members with avatars, allowing role changes (including owner transfer with confirmation) and swipe-to-kick removal, with optimistic updates and API rollback on failure.

nodes.name: MemberRolesModal
nodes.summary: Modal component that fetches calendar members, changes roles optimistically with server rollback, confirms destructive owner transfers and member kicks, and refreshes the calendars store afterwards.

nodes.name: useModalAnimation.ts
nodes.summary: Shared Reanimated hook powering all bottom-sheet modals: timed enter/exit slide-and-fade, keyboard-height tracking, and a pan gesture with rubber-banding and swipe-to-dismiss.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately uses timing curves for programmatic enter/exit and reserves springs for gesture release, where finger velocity makes spring physics feel natural.

nodes.name: useModalAnimation
nodes.summary: Custom hook returning animated styles, a pan gesture, and a handleClose function; drives slide/fade shared values on visibility changes, rides the keyboard, and dismisses when dragged past a threshold.

nodes.name: useCalendarsStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store holding the calendar list and visibility state (active set, solo mode), with API-backed add/update/remove actions and merge-based local updates that preserve per-user fields like role when partial payloads arrive.
nodes.languageNotes: Update actions merge into existing calendar objects instead of replacing them, because server and SSE payloads omit per-user fields (role, provider) and a replace would silently drop edit rights.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Entry point of the db package that creates the Drizzle ORM database client wired to the full schema and exports it as the shared `db` instance.
nodes.languageNotes: Drizzle client is constructed once and shared across the monorepo, making it a de facto singleton.

nodes.name: schema.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle ORM schema defining all database tables for Musubi: auth tables (user, session, account, verification), calendar domain tables (calendars, events, calendarMembers, calendarEvents, eventUsers, calendarInvites), user profile tables (userAvatars, userSettings, userStatus), and external sync tables (externalCalendars, externalEvents, caldavAccounts), plus their relation definitions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Drizzle's `relations()` helpers alongside table definitions to enable the relational query API (db.query.*.findMany).

nodes.name: events.ts
nodes.summary: Data-access layer for events: creation with multi-calendar linking, lookups (origin, linked calendars, by id), updates, per-user event feed with since-based delta sync, soft delete via deletedAt, and purge of old soft-deleted rows.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses soft deletion (deletedAt timestamp) so clients can sync deletions incrementally, with purgeDeletedEvents as the hard-delete garbage collector.

nodes.name: google.ts
nodes.summary: Queries against the Better Auth `account` table for Google integration: checks whether a user has a Google account with calendar scope, lists linked Google account IDs, fetches the refresh token, and clears stored Google tokens.

nodes.name: createEvent
nodes.summary: Inserts a new event, records the creator in eventUsers, and links the event to every calendar ID passed in via the calendarEvents join table.

nodes.name: getEventOrigin
nodes.summary: Returns an event's origin calendar ID and creator ID, used for ownership and permission checks.

nodes.name: getEventCalendars
nodes.summary: Lists the calendar IDs an event is linked to via the calendarEvents join table.

nodes.name: getEvent
nodes.summary: Fetches a single event row by its ID.

nodes.name: updateEvent
nodes.summary: Updates an event row with a partial event object and returns the updated row.

nodes.name: getUsersEvents
nodes.summary: Returns all events visible to a user by joining calendarMembers through calendarEvents to events, optionally filtered to changes after a `since` timestamp for delta sync (including soft-deleted rows when syncing).

nodes.name: purgeDeletedEvents
nodes.summary: Hard-deletes events that were soft-deleted before a cutoff date, acting as the garbage collector for tombstones.

nodes.name: removeEvent
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes an event by setting its deletedAt timestamp so clients can pick up the deletion during sync.

nodes.name: googleCheck
nodes.summary: Checks whether a user has a linked Google account whose OAuth scope includes Google Calendar access.

nodes.name: getGoogleAccountIDs
nodes.summary: Returns the account IDs of a user's linked Google accounts that carry the Google Calendar scope, supporting multi-account sync.

nodes.name: getGoogleRefreshToken
nodes.summary: Fetches the stored Google OAuth refresh token for a user's linked Google account.

nodes.name: cleanUsersGoogleTokens
nodes.summary: Nullifies a user's stored Google refresh token, access token, expiry, and scope, effectively disconnecting the Google integration.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Email delivery utility that creates a nodemailer SMTP transport and exposes a single sendEmail helper used for transactional mail like password resets.

nodes.name: sendEmail
nodes.summary: Sends an HTML email to a recipient via a nodemailer SMTP transport, logging success or failure without throwing on delivery errors.

nodes.name: password_reset.ts
nodes.summary: HTML template generator for the password reset email, producing an inline-styled message with the user's name, reset link, and expiry notice.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a single large template literal with inline CSS for email-client compatibility.

nodes.name: getPasswordResetHtml
nodes.summary: Builds the full HTML body for a password reset email, interpolating the user name, reset URL, expiry duration, and current year into an inline-styled template.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Central Better Auth configuration exporting the shared auth instance: Drizzle/Postgres adapter, email+password with reset-email delivery, Google social login with account linking, Expo trusted origins, and a database hook that creates a personal calendar for every new user.
nodes.languageNotes: Single betterAuth() configuration object; behavior is defined via inline async callbacks (sendResetPassword, user.create.after hook) rather than standalone functions.

nodes.name: calendar.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the shared Zod schemas for calendars (CalendarSchema, CalendarWithEventsSchema) including membership, role, default-calendar flag, and external sync-provider fields, plus the providerFlavor helper that maps a calendar's sync origin to a display brand.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zod schemas as the single source of truth, deriving TypeScript types via z.infer.

nodes.name: providerFlavor
nodes.summary: Determines the display brand for a calendar's sync origin, returning "apple" for CalDAV calendars pointed at icloud.com and the raw provider name otherwise.

nodes.name: event.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the shared Zod schema and inferred type for calendar events, covering timing (with date coercion), multi-calendar membership, origin calendar for edit rights, recurrence, and cancellation state.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses z.coerce.date() so string timestamps from the API deserialize into Date objects at the validation boundary.

nodes.name: user.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the minimal shared Zod schema and inferred type for users (id, name, validated email, optional avatar image) used across the client and API.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the Musubi Express API: installs pnpm workspace dependencies filtered to @musubi/api, copies the api app plus its workspace packages, then runs drizzle-kit database migrations before starting the server via tsx on a configurable port (default 7531).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pnpm --filter with a package.json-only COPY layer in the deps stage so dependency installation is cached independently of source changes; the CMD chains database migration and server startup at container boot.

nodes.name: base
nodes.summary: Base stage on node:22-alpine that enables corepack and pins pnpm 10.33.2, setting /repo as the workspace root.

nodes.name: deps
nodes.summary: Dependency stage that copies only lockfile, workspace config, and per-package package.json files, then runs a frozen-lockfile pnpm install filtered to @musubi/api and its workspace dependencies for optimal layer caching.

nodes.name: runner
nodes.summary: Runtime stage that copies the api app and its workspace packages (db, auth, types, config), sets NODE_ENV=production, exposes the API port, and boots by running drizzle-kit migrations then starting src/index.ts with tsx.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Docker build for the @musubi/docs package: installs pnpm, builds the Astro docs site, and serves it via the Node SSR entry on port 4321.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-stage build copying the whole monorepo and using pnpm --filter to build only the docs workspace; no multi-stage slimming.

nodes.name: runtime
nodes.summary: Build/runtime stage based on node:lts that installs dependencies, builds the docs, and runs the Astro SSR server entry on port 4321.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context exclusion list that keeps node_modules, build artifacts, and local files out of the API image builds.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template of environment variables for running Musubi — Postgres credentials, DATABASE_URL, API port, Better Auth secrets, SMTP mail settings, Google OAuth client IDs, and the CalDAV encryption key.
nodes.languageNotes: Declares 17 environment variables consumed by the API server, Better Auth, mailer, Google Sign-In, and CalDAV credential encryption.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and getting-started documentation covering Musubi's purpose, current features, sync providers, roadmap, how to try it, self-hosting instructions, architecture, and contribution guidelines.

nodes.name: docker-compose.selfhost.yml
nodes.summary: Generic self-hosting Compose stack that builds the API image and runs it alongside an embedded Postgres 17 database, publishing the API port on the host for use behind a reverse proxy.
nodes.languageNotes: Publishes the API port to the host (ports:) and waits on a Postgres healthcheck via depends_on service_healthy.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Dokploy-targeted Compose stack that builds and runs the API against an embedded Postgres 17, attaching to an external dokploy-network and only exposing the API port for Dokploy's reverse proxy.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses expose (not ports) and an external dokploy-network, so it requires the Dokploy reverse proxy rather than a bare docker compose up.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm workspace definition listing apps/* and packages/* members and forcing a hoisted node_modules layout required by Expo/Metro tooling, plus dependency overrides and Expo version exclusions.
nodes.languageNotes: nodeLinker: hoisted is set because Expo config plugins and Metro assume a flat node_modules; minimumReleaseAgeExclude pins specific Expo SDK 56 packages.

nodes.name: turbo.json
nodes.summary: Turborepo task pipeline configuration defining the dev and build tasks orchestrated across the monorepo workspaces.

nodes.name: 0000_solid_captain_britain.sql
nodes.summary: Initial Drizzle migration for the client-side SQLite database, creating the notifications_table with id, identifier, eventID, and triggerDate columns.
nodes.languageNotes: Drizzle-generated SQLite migration; backtick-quoted identifiers indicate SQLite/MySQL dialect.

nodes.name: notifications_table
nodes.summary: Local notifications table storing scheduled event reminders keyed by identifier and eventID with a trigger date.

nodes.name: 0001_next_stark_industries.sql
nodes.summary: Drizzle migration adding the events and sync_meta tables and rebuilding notifications_table for the client-side offline event store.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the SQLite table-rebuild pattern (__new_ temp table) that Drizzle emits for column changes since SQLite lacks full ALTER support.

nodes.name: events
nodes.summary: Core local events table storing calendar events with timing, recurrence, organizer, location, and calendar association fields for offline access.

nodes.name: sync_meta
nodes.summary: Key-value metadata table tracking sync state for the client offline data layer.

nodes.name: 0002_loving_psynapse.sql
nodes.summary: Drizzle migration adding an originCalendarID text column to the events table to track each event's source calendar.

nodes.name: 0003_classy_vermin.sql
nodes.summary: Drizzle migration adding an offsetMinutes column (default 15) to notifications_table for configurable reminder lead time.

nodes.name: 0000_snapshot.json
nodes.summary: Drizzle-Kit schema snapshot for migration 0000 of the client's local SQLite database, capturing the initial notifications_table definition.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated drizzle-kit snapshot (SQLite dialect) describing table columns, indexes and foreign keys at a point in migration history.

nodes.name: 0001_snapshot.json
nodes.summary: Drizzle-Kit schema snapshot for migration 0001, adding the events and sync_meta tables alongside notifications_table to the client's local SQLite database.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated drizzle-kit snapshot; prevId links it to the 0000 snapshot forming an ordered migration chain.

nodes.name: 0002_snapshot.json
nodes.summary: Drizzle-Kit schema snapshot for migration 0002 of the client's local SQLite database (events, notifications_table, sync_meta), reflecting the schema after the 0002 migration.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated drizzle-kit snapshot chained to 0001 via prevId.

nodes.name: 0003_snapshot.json
nodes.summary: Drizzle-Kit schema snapshot for migration 0003, the latest recorded state of the client's local SQLite database (events, notifications_table, sync_meta).
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated drizzle-kit snapshot; latest in the chain, linked to 0002 via prevId.

nodes.name: _journal.json
nodes.summary: Drizzle-Kit migration journal listing the ordered set of applied migrations (0000 through 0003) with their tags and timestamps for the client's local SQLite database.
nodes.languageNotes: Master index consumed by drizzle-kit and the migrations bundle to determine which SQL migrations have been generated and in what order.

nodes.name: eas.json
nodes.summary: Expo Application Services build configuration defining development, preview, and production build profiles plus submit settings for the mobile client.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the @musubi/client Expo/React Native app declaring dependencies, dev tooling, and scripts for starting, building, and linting the mobile client.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses expo-router/entry as the main entry point; scripts wrap the Expo CLI (start, run:android/ios, lint).

nodes.name: 0000_square_dexter_bennett.sql
nodes.summary: Initial Drizzle migration establishing the full baseline schema: Better Auth tables (user, session, account, verification) plus the core calendar domain (calendars, events, members, invites, junctions).
nodes.languageNotes: Drizzle-generated migration; foreign keys wrapped in DO $$ blocks for idempotent application, with supporting indexes on user_id and identifier columns.

nodes.name: account
nodes.summary: Better Auth OAuth/credential account table storing provider tokens, scopes, and password hashes linked to a user.

nodes.name: calendar_events
nodes.summary: Junction table linking events to the calendars they appear on.

nodes.name: calendar_invites
nodes.summary: Shareable calendar invite tokens with expiry and max-use limits.

nodes.name: calendar_members
nodes.summary: Membership join table associating users with calendars they can access.

nodes.name: calendars
nodes.summary: Core calendars table with creator, name, and color, forming the shared-calendar backbone of the app.

nodes.name: event_users
nodes.summary: Junction table linking events to their participating users.

nodes.name: events
nodes.summary: Core events table with creator, name, color, and start/end timestamps; extended by later migrations with all-day, recurrence, location, and soft-delete columns.

nodes.name: session
nodes.summary: Better Auth session table tracking tokens, IP address, and user agent per authenticated user.

nodes.name: user
nodes.summary: Better Auth user table holding name, email, verification status, and profile image.

nodes.name: verification
nodes.summary: Better Auth verification table storing time-limited identifier/value tokens for email verification and similar flows.

nodes.name: 0001_funny_nekra.sql
nodes.summary: Migration creating the user_settings table for per-user preferences (kanji display, default view, week start).

nodes.name: user_settings
nodes.summary: Per-user preferences table (show_kanji, default_calendar_view, week_starts_on); grown across later migrations with notifications, locale, theme, and onboarding flags.

nodes.name: 0002_flaky_natasha_romanoff.sql
nodes.summary: Migration extending the events table with all-day, description, location, cancellation, organizer, recurrence, and URL columns.

nodes.name: 0003_medical_sebastian_shaw.sql
nodes.summary: Migration creating the user_status table tracking sponsor and premium flags per user.

nodes.name: user_status
nodes.summary: Tracks per-user sponsor and premium entitlement flags.

nodes.name: 0004_legal_maestro.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the notifications_on_by_default flag to user_settings.

nodes.name: 0005_green_vin_gonzales.sql
nodes.summary: Migration dropping the default on the events id column.

nodes.name: 0006_mushy_mac_gargan.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a time_locale column (default en-UK) to user_settings.

nodes.name: 0007_windy_doorman.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding first-generation Google sync tables (google_calendars, google_events); later superseded and dropped by migration 0010.

nodes.name: google_calendars
nodes.summary: First-generation Google Calendar sync mapping (calendar_id to google_calendar_id with sync token); dropped in migration 0010 in favor of the generic external_calendars table.

nodes.name: google_events
nodes.summary: First-generation Google event sync mapping; dropped in migration 0010 in favor of the generic external_events table.

nodes.name: 0008_broken_penance.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a user_id foreign key and a per-user uniqueness constraint to google_calendars.

nodes.name: 0009_neat_meteorite.sql
nodes.summary: Migration introducing the generic multi-provider sync tables external_calendars and external_events, replacing the Google-specific approach.

nodes.name: external_calendars
nodes.summary: Generic provider-agnostic mapping between local calendars and external provider calendars, carrying a sync cursor for incremental sync.

nodes.name: external_events
nodes.summary: Generic provider-agnostic mapping between local events and external provider events, tracking etag for change detection.

nodes.name: 0010_high_bloodstorm.sql
nodes.summary: Migration dropping the deprecated google_calendars and google_events tables now that generic external sync tables exist.

nodes.name: 0011_boring_mentallo.sql
nodes.summary: Migration creating the caldav_accounts table for storing CalDAV server credentials per user.

nodes.name: caldav_accounts
nodes.summary: Stores CalDAV connection details (server URL, username, encrypted password) per user for calendar synchronization.

nodes.name: 0012_late_anthem.sql
nodes.summary: Migration reworking sync uniqueness constraints and adding an account_id link on external_calendars to support multiple accounts per provider.

nodes.name: 0013_zippy_baron_zemo.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding an account_label column to external_calendars for display of the source account.

nodes.name: 0014_robust_sister_grimm.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a deleted_at timestamp to events, enabling soft-delete semantics.

nodes.name: 0015_deep_longshot.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a role column (default editor) to calendar_members, introducing per-member permissions.

nodes.name: 0016_supreme_puppet_master.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding an origin_calendar_id foreign key on events referencing calendars, with ON DELETE SET NULL.

nodes.name: 0017_fine_brood.sql
nodes.summary: Migration changing the calendar_members role default to viewer and adding a theme column to user_settings.

nodes.name: 0018_cute_blue_shield.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding an is_default flag to calendars and an onboarded flag to user_settings.

nodes.name: 0019_lean_mattie_franklin.sql
nodes.summary: Migration creating the user_avatars table for storing binary avatar image data with mime type.

nodes.name: user_avatars
nodes.summary: Stores user avatar images as binary data with associated mime type.

nodes.name: 0020_opposite_ulik.sql
nodes.summary: Drizzle migration that changes the default value of user_settings.default_calendar_view to 'month'.
